title: boronia clavata subsp. grandis
intro: Boronia clavata subsp. grandis is a subspecies of flowering plant in the citrus family Rutaceae, native to Australia, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and woody stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Boronia clavata subsp. grandis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
